🔩 Escalator Parts Library

Parts grouped by Escalator Type → Model Variant → EG Assembly. Each import for a unique spec is stored separately.

📋 Excel Import — How it works:
Parts are stored per Escalator Type + Model + Controller + Motor KW combination.
Same spec imported again? → You'll get a warning and can choose to overwrite.
Different spec? → Stored as a separate variant under the same Escalator Type.
Set Escalator Type, Model, Controller & Motor KW above, then click 📤 Import Excel.

USER'S GUIDE
BOM VALIDATOR  ·  JOHNSON LIFTS AND ESCALATORS
● LIVE REFERENCE
This guide explains every feature of the BOM Validator system — from setting job parameters and loading your BOM file, to validating parts, reviewing missing items, managing the parts library, and exporting reports. Follow each section in order for a complete walkthrough.
① Overview ② Interface Layout ③ BOM Checker ④ Part Search ⑤ Material Master ⑥ Parts Library ⑦ Roles & Access ⑧ Workflow ⑨ Quick Reference ⑩ Entry Cable Calculations 🏗️ System Architecture
01 / 13
⚡ BOM VALIDATION SYSTEM · JOHNSON LIFTS AND ESCALATORS
BOM
VALIDATOR
Step-by-step walkthrough — how to validate your Electrical BOM for any project, from job setup to export.
DEVELOPED BY  ·  NANDAKUMAR.M
ELECTRICAL DESIGNING AND DEVELOPMENT
JOHNSON LIFTS AND ESCALATORS  ·  ORAGADAM
SECTION 01 · OVERVIEW
What is BOM Validation?
📋
BILL OF MATERIALS
Complete list of electrical parts, components & assemblies required to build an escalator — part numbers, quantities & UOM.
✅
STANDARD MASTER
Approved reference database per EG assembly (EG01–EG29). Sets the gold standard for every job BOM regardless of project.
⚡
VALIDATION CHECK
Compares incoming job BOM against standard — flags mismatches, missing parts, duplicate entries & non-standard items instantly.
SECTION 02 · STATUS FLAGS
5 Validation Status Flags
✔ MATCH
Part number exists in Standard Master with matching quantity. No action needed.
002 114 1056 × 2
⚡ QTY DIFF
Part exists in standard but input quantity differs from the standard quantity.
002 114 1091 × 3 (Std: 2)
⚠ DUPLICATE
Same part number entered more than once in the current BOM. First entry wins.
002 114 1056 entered twice
✘ NOT IN STD
Part number not found in Standard Master — non-standard or unknown part.
999 000 0011 (no match)
● MISSING
Standard part not yet found in the job BOM. Shown in the red Missing Parts sidebar.
Sidebar → red list
SECTION 03 · STEP-BY-STEP
Step 1 & 2 — Job Setup
1 Set Job Parameters
In the Job Bar at the top of Tab 1, fill in:
Job No — e.g. ESC-2025-0391
Job Type — Metro / RDSO / Commercial
KW Rating — 7.5 / 11 / 18.5 / 22 / 37 KW
Escalator Model — DMRC / CMRL / BIAL…
Controller — Inside / Outside / Custom
2 Log In (Tab 3)
Navigate to Tab 3 → Standard Material Master. Enter your username and password.
ℹ️ Login is required only to upload or edit standard data. BOM checking and export work for all users without login.
SECTION 03 · STEP-BY-STEP
Step 3 — Load BOM File
3 Upload BOM Excel
Tab 1 → click Load BOM File (.xls/.xlsx)
App auto-detects EG sections present in the file
A modal appears — Select EG Section to validate (e.g. EG29)
Click Load & Validate → all parts are instantly checked
💡 Or use Manual Single Entry — type Part No + Qty and click Validate.
EXPECTED EXCEL FORMAT
Sl.No Part No. Matl Id. 1002 114 1056113801 2002 114 1091113904 3002 114 1103114022 Qty UOM REMARKS 2NOSEG29 1NOSEG29 4MTREG23
SECTION 04 · EG07 — SKIRTING & BALUSTRADE SUPPORT ASSEMBLY
EG07 Validation Example
#Part NoMaterial IDDescriptionQtyUOMStatus
01002 108 1012107143CONNECT PART-YFEA0550188NOS✔ MATCH
02005 108 1033178317SUPPORT LH - YFEN06601—NOS✘ MISSING
03005 108 1049178333SKIRT SUPPORT LH - YFEJ067011NOS⚠ DUPLICATE
04005 108 1050178334SKIRT SUPPORT RH - YFEJ067023NOS🔵 QTY DIFF
📌 EG07 = Skirting & Balustrade Support Assembly. Inclined rail supports and skirt panels — both LH and RH must be present.
SECTION 05 · EG11 — STEP & STEP CHAIN ASSEMBLY
EG11 Validation Example
#Part NoMaterial IDDescriptionQtyUOMStatus
01005 106 1220164165METRO STEP ASSY 1000MM - YFKN07301-L96NOS🔵 QTY DIFF
02005 106 1224186663STEP CHAIN ASSY (180KN) - YFKJ05201-L94NOS✔ MATCH
03001 303 110029788SPRING WASHER 12 - B0330900—NOS⛔ NOT IN BOM
04002 104 1031107298COVER PLATE-YFGA069012NOS➕ EXTRA PART
📌 EG11 = Step & Step Chain Assembly. BFL Part No. N0039 applies to Metro Step. Verify chain tension and washer counts at installation.
SECTION 06 · EG29 — ELECTRICAL / CONTROL ASSEMBLY
EG29 Validation Example
#Part NoMaterial IDDescriptionQtyUOMStatus
01002 114 1061112310V3-2LINE LCD DISPLAY BOARD2NOS⚠ DUPLICATE
02002 114 1101115406VAL MS 320/40/3+1/FM-GY-28006861NOS✔ MATCH
03002 114 1570146191MCB 1POLE 3A 24VDC - A9N1P03C SCHNEIDER3NOS🔵 QTY DIFF
04002 114 1569146190PHASE MONITOR - RM17TG20 SCHNEIDER—NOS✘ MISSING
📌 EG29 = Electrical / Control Assembly. Includes display boards, safety controller and Schneider protection devices. All 4 parts matched Standard BOM.

⚡ BOM VALIDATOR — JOHNSON LIFTS AND ESCALATORS
Electrical BOM Validation System · EG01–EG29 · Browser-based · No installation required
⚡
System Architecture & Data Flow
LIVE PROCESS FLOW
Johnson Lifts & Escalators · Oragadam
BOM Validator & Cable Calc — Process Data Flow
SYSTEM IDLE
📂 Upload
⚙️ Parse
📊 Index
🔎 Validate
🚩 Flag
⚡ Calculate
📤 Export
💾 Persist
STEP 01 · DATA COLLECTION
Excel BOM Upload
Engineer selects the job Excel file. The XLSX Import Processor reads binary stream, parses every sheet row, and maps columns to internal schema.
📂
XLSX File
FileReader API
⚙️
XLSX Parser
xlsx-js-style
📋
Raw Rows
Array of objects
DATA STREAM
file.name "DMRC_37KW_BOM_Job2024.xlsx"
sheets ["Sheet1", "BOM", "Cable"]
rows_detected 0
status "PARSED OK"
Layer 3 · Data Processing
STEP 02 · DATA NORMALIZATION
Parse & Normalize
Raw rows are trimmed, uppercased, validated for required fields (PN, QT, UM). Invalid rows are flagged. Clean records flow into the runtime verList[] array.
🔡
Row Trimmer
Trim · uppercase
✅
Field Validator
PN · QT · UM check
📦
verList[ ]
Runtime BOM array
DATA STREAM
{ partNo "EJ12-0034",
  inputQty 4,
  uom "NOS",
  status "VALID" }
Layer 3 · Data Processing → verList[ ]
STEP 03 · MATERIAL INDEXING
Build Search Index
The Material Search Engine builds a live hash-map indexed by Part No (PN), Material ID (MI), and Description (DS). This powers instant sub-millisecond lookups during validation and search.
🗂️
stdData[ ]
Master BOM — EG01–29
🔍
Hash Index
PN · MI · DS maps
⚡
Lookup Ready
<1ms response
INDEX TABLE
pnIndex["EJ12-0034"] → {QT:4,EG:"EG12"}
miIndex["EJ12-0034"] → "CABLE GLAND"
egGroups["EG12"] → [34 parts]
indexSize 0 entries
Layer 2 · Material Search Engine
STEP 04 · BOM VALIDATION
Validate Each Part
Every part in verList[] is cross-referenced against stdData[]. The BOM Validator assigns one of 5 status codes — MATCH, QTY_MISMATCH, NOT_IN_STD, DUPLICATE, or MISSING — per part number.
📋
BOM Validator
verList vs stdData
⚖️
Comparator
PN lookup · qty diff
🏷️
Status Flags
5 result codes
✅ EXACT MATCH
🟡 QTY MISMATCH
🔴 NOT IN STD
🟣 DUPLICATE PN
🟠 MISSING PART
Layer 2 · BOM Validator · Standard Comparison Module
STEP 05 · ANOMALY DETECTION
Flag & Detect
Three specialist sub-engines run in parallel: Duplicate Detection scans for repeated PNs, Extra Part Detection flags non-standard parts, and Qty Mismatch Analyzer computes quantity deltas vs the Standard.
🔁
Dup Detector
cnt[PN] > 1 check
📐
Qty Analyzer
inputQty ≠ stdQty
🚫
Extra Parts
!stdData.find(PN)
DETECTION OUTPUT
duplicates 2 found
qty_mismatches 5 found
extra_parts 3 not in std
missing_parts 7 from std
Layer 2 · Duplicate Detection · Qty Analyzer · Extra Part Engine
STEP 06 · CABLE CALCULATION
Entry Cable Calculator
Parallel to BOM validation, the Entry Cable Calculator takes motor KW, supply voltage, power factor, efficiency, and cable run length. It computes full-load current, derating factor, and recommends cable cross-section and entry gland size per IS standards.
⚡
Motor Params
KW · V · PF · η
📐
FLC Engine
I = P/(√3·V·PF·η)
🔌
Cable Sizer
mm² · gland size
CALCULATION OUTPUT
FLC 74.8 A
design_current 93.5 A (×1.25)
cable_size 35 mm²
gland_size "M40"
Layer 2 · Entry Cable Calculator
STEP 07 · REPORT GENERATION
Export Report
The Validation Result Engine aggregates all flags and produces downloadable outputs. Engineers can export the full validation table, missing parts list, or cable calculation as XLSX or PDF — all generated in-browser via blob URLs.
📊
Result Engine
Aggregator · chart
📗
XLSX Builder
xlsx-js-style blob
📄
PDF Renderer
window.print blob
📗 BOM Validation Report .xlsx
📗 Missing Parts List .xlsx
📄 Validation Report .pdf
📘 Cable Calc Report .xlsx
Layer 1 · Validation Dashboard · Export System
STEP 08 · OFFLINE PERSISTENCE
Store & Persist
The Offline Storage Manager uses the safeLS() wrapper around localStorage to persist the Standard BOM, user sessions, active EG filter, and cable calculation jobs — all in the browser with zero server dependency.
💾
safeLS( )
localStorage wrapper
🗄️
Browser Storage
localStorage · session
📴
Offline Ready
No internet req.
STORAGE KEYS
B_stdData → Standard BOM JSON
B_cu → Current user session
B_activeEG → Active EG filter
B_ccJobs → Cable calc saved jobs
Layer 4 · Offline Storage Manager · Browser Persistence
Workflow Progress
Step 1 of 8
⚡ Vanilla JS 📴 Offline-First 🔒 Zero-Server 🏗️ Single HTML File 📊 xlsx-js-style 💾 localStorage API 🔍 Chart.js Donut 📄 Print-to-PDF
🗂️
1  ·  System Overview

The BOM Validator is a self-contained, browser-based tool for verifying electrical Bill-of-Materials for Johnson Lifts & Escalators projects (e.g. DMRC 37 KW VFD installations). It runs entirely from a single HTML file — no server, no installation, no internet required.

⚡
Real-Time Validation
Instantly checks each part against the Standard Material Master
🔴
Missing Parts Sidebar
Live pinned sidebar shows every standard part not yet found in your BOM
🟡
Duplicate Detection
Flags duplicate part numbers with jump-to-duplicate navigation
🔍
Part Search
Full-text search across Part No, Material ID and Description
📤
PDF & Excel Export
One-click export of results, missing parts, and the full parts library
🔑
Role-Based Access
Admin · Approver · User roles control editing and deletion rights
🖥️
2  ·  Interface Layout
① Header Bar
Company logo · animated project title ticker · live clock. Confirms the active project at a glance.
② Tab Navigation
Four tabs: BOM Checker | Part Search | Material Master | All Parts. Click any tab to switch panels instantly.
③ Job Info Bar
Escalator Type · KW Rating · Model · Controller. These fields filter the correct standard BOM for validation.
④ KPI Stats Row
Five live counters: Standard Parts · Parts Checked · Matches · Duplicates · Missing. Updated on every action.
⬡
3  ·  BOM Checker & Missing Parts (Tab 1)
1
Set Job Parameters
Fill in Escalator Type, KW Rating, Model and Controller Location in the Job Info Bar. These control which standard parts are used for validation. Always set these before loading a BOM.
2
Load the BOM File
Click Load BOM File → select your Excel (.xls / .xlsx) escalator child-BOM report. The system auto-detects EG sections (e.g. EG29 – Electrical Assembly). In the pop-up modal, choose which EG section to validate — or All EG Sections — then click Load Section.
💡 Flat BOM formats (no EG headers) are supported — all parts load automatically.
3
Add / Enter Parts
Single Add: type Part No + Qty → click Add.  Bulk Add: paste many Part Nos (one per line) and Quantities into the text areas → click Add All.
4
Read Validation Results
✔ MATCH
Part found in standard master
✘ MISSING
Part not in the current standard
⚠ DUPLICATE
Same Part No appears twice
🔵 QTY DIFF
Qty differs from standard
5
Missing Parts Sidebar
The red-bordered sidebar on the right lists every standard part absent from the verified BOM. Use Export Missing (PDF) or Export Missing (Excel) for procurement follow-up.
6
Export Full Results
Export Results PDF — full validation report.  Export Excel — full verification list as XLSX. Both include job metadata and KPI summary.
🔍
4  ·  Part Search (Tab 2)

A global search panel across all parts in the Standard Material Master. Type any keyword — Part No, Material ID, or Description — results appear instantly (live filter, no button needed).

Best used for:
• Verifying a part exists in the master before adding it to a BOM
• Cross-referencing Material ID ↔ Part No
• Checking UOM and standard quantity before entering a BOM line
📋
5  ·  Standard Material Master (Tab 3)

The administrative hub. Login required. Contains three sub-sections:

5A · User Management Admin Only
View all users and roles. Create new users (Username, Full Name, Password, Role). Delete users with double-approval safety.
Password Role
••••Approver
••••User
••••Admin
⚠ Change passwords after first login. Admin has full access including user management.
5B · Upload Standard Parts from Excel
Select Type, Model, Controller, KW and EG Section → upload .xls/.xlsx → the system imports only parts from the chosen section. "Replace ALL existing" checkbox (default ON) clears old data before importing. A history table records all past uploads.
💡 EG29 = Electrical Assembly. Choose All EG Sections for a complete import.
5C · Manual Standard Parts Entry
Paste data directly into six text areas: Part Nos · Material IDs · Descriptions · Std Qty · UOM · Remarks (one entry per line, all six columns must match line-for-line). Click Save to Standard. An approval step applies depending on your role.
🔩
6  ·  All Escalator Parts Library (Tab 4)

A comprehensive hierarchical library of all escalator parts across every EG assembly — not filtered by the current job. Use this as the master reference for all escalator components.

📂 Browse & Search
Search by Part No, Description or Material ID. Filter by EG section. Expand/collapse assembly groups (tree view).
✏️ Add / Edit / Delete
Click + to add a part. Click edit icon to modify. Delete requires Approver or Admin role + confirmation.
📋 Bulk Add
Paste six columns simultaneously (Part No, Mat ID, Description, Qty, UOM, Remarks) to add many parts at once.
📤 Export
Export Library PDF and Export Library Excel buttons at the top. Search/filter is applied to the export.
🔑
7  ·  Roles & Access Control
Feature 👤 User ✅ Approver 🔑 Admin
View / Search parts✔✔✔
Add parts to BOM / Export✔✔✔
Upload / Manual standard parts✔✔✔
Approve part changes✘✔✔
Delete parts (library / standard)✘✔✔
User management✘✘✔
🔄

⚡
9  ·  Quick Reference Card
Task Where / How
Start a new BOM checkTab 1 → Set Job Params → Load BOM File
Upload standard partsTab 3 → Upload Excel section
Search for any partTab 2 → type keyword in search box
Bulk add BOM entriesTab 1 → Bulk Add text areas → Add All
Find a duplicateTab 1 → click "Jump to Dup" link in the row
See missing partsTab 1 → right-hand Missing Parts Sidebar
Export verification reportTab 1 → Export Results PDF / Export Excel
Export missing parts listTab 1 → Sidebar → Export PDF / Export Excel
Add a part to the libraryTab 4 → + Add New Part button
Create a new userTab 3 → User Management → Add User (Admin only)
Reload a recent jobTab 1 → Recent Jobs section → click job card
🔌
10  ·  Entry Cable Calculation

The Entry Cable Calc tab (🔌) automatically calculates the cable quantities and costs for MCC-TMB entry cables for each escalator job, based on the entry type (TOP or BOTTOM), motor KW, span, and site measurement.

📥 INPUT FIELDS
  • Job No — unique job identifier
  • Job Name — optional descriptive name
  • Entry — TOP or BOTTOM
  • Motor KW — 7.5 / 11 / 15 / 18.5 / 22 / 30 / 37
  • Span (mm) — escalator span in millimetres
  • Site Measurement (mm) — cable run to MCC
  • Controller Location — OUTSIDE / INSIDE
📤 CABLE OUTPUTS
  • MT01 — Control Harness (30×0.75)
  • MT02 — Control Cable (12×0.75)
  • MT03 — Auxiliary Cable (4×1.5)
  • C01 — Signal Cable (2P×0.75+SH)
  • MT04 — Power Cable (4-core)
  • E0 — Earth Cable Motor
  • E01 — Earth Cable Duct
🔢 LENGTH FORMULAS
TOP entry: All cables = Site ÷ 1000 m
  (E01 = Span × 1.2 ÷ 1000 m)

BOTTOM entry:
  MT01/MT02/MT04 = (Span×1.2 + Site) ÷ 1000
  MT03/C01/E0 = Site ÷ 1000
  E01 = Span × 1.2 ÷ 1000
🛠 HOW TO USE
  1. Click the 🔌 Entry Cable Calc tab.
  2. Fill in the job parameters (Job No, Entry, KW, Span, Site Measurement, Controller).
  3. Click ▶ CALCULATE — cable lengths, rates and totals appear instantly.
  4. Click 💾 SAVE JOB to add this job to the saved list.
  5. You can also bulk import multiple jobs by pasting from Excel — required columns: Job No · Job Name · Entry · Span · Site Measurement · Motor KW.
  6. Select saved jobs using checkboxes and click ⬇ DOWNLOAD to export an Excel report.
📊 EXCEL EXPORT STRUCTURE
The downloaded Excel file contains multiple sheets:
Sheet 1 — Summary: All jobs in one table (SL No, Job No, Entry, KW, Span, Site, Total Rate).
Sheet 2 — Entry Cable Details: Full report matching the uploaded screenshot format — PPC Scope, Design Scope, Purchase Scope columns with color-coded headers.
Per-Job Sheets: Individual cable breakdown for each job — cable ID, description, drawing, spec, length, rate and price — with full borders and alternating row colours.
BOM VALIDATOR  ·  JOHNSON LIFTS AND ESCALATORS
For internal use · All data stored locally in browser · No internet required

About the Company
Johnson Lifts Private Limited is one of India's leading manufacturers and suppliers of elevators, escalators, and moving walkways. With over five decades of engineering excellence, Johnson Lifts serves metro rail projects, commercial complexes, hospitals, airports, and residential buildings across India and international markets. The company is renowned for its robust electrical and mechanical design, stringent quality standards, and end-to-end project execution capability — from manufacturing at Oragadam to installation and after-sales service nationwide.
